titlestmt.titleproper: Ethel Granger archive of photographs and ephemera7772
titlestmt.author: Compiled by RMC Staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2022-11-27 23:24:48 UTC.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.unittitle: Ethel Granger archive of photographs and ephemera
did.abstract: Includes cardboard box addressed to Ethel Granger from Kurt Ingerl, containing a ring-binder with 87 gelatin silver prints, ca. 1970. (Ingerl, an Austrian artist, used these photographs as the basis for a series of sculptures.) 2 c-type (chromogenic color) and 91 gelatin silver prints showing candid photographs of Granger from ca. 1933-ca. 1980; a pencil sketch of Granger signed by J.G. Wolcott, 1957 and 5 photographic reproductions of other works by Wolcott; 67 gelatin silver prints of other like-minded individuals; "Tiny Waist Stories," typed manuscript (4 leaves); two of Granger's earrings (which appear in some of the photographs), a belt buckle, and a button.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524rareref@cornell.eduhttp://rmc.library.cornell.edu

bioghist.head: BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
bioghist.p: Ethel Granger was a British woman who, through the practice of tightlacing, achieved a waist measurement of thirteen inches. She was listed in the Guinness Book of World Records as having the smallest waist on record until 1998, when the category was changed to "smallest waist on a living person."

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Includes cardboard box addressed to Ethel Granger from Kurt Ingerl, containing a ring-binder with 87 gelatin silver prints, ca. 1970. (Ingerl, an Austrian artist, used these photographs as the basis for a series of sculptures.) 2 c-type (chromogenic color) and 91 gelatin silver prints showing candid photographs of Granger from ca. 1933-ca. 1980; a pencil sketch of Granger signed by J.G. Wolcott, 1957 and 5 photographic reproductions of other works by Wolcott; 67 gelatin silver prints of other like-minded individuals; "Tiny Waist Stories," typed manuscript (4 leaves); two of Granger's earrings (which appear in some of the photographs), a belt buckle, and a button.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Ethel Granger Archive of Photographs and Ephemera, #7772. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
